_note: Auto-managed by ci-reward.mjs --freeze. Each series here must keep fetching ok with >= minPoints, or CI flags a regression. The corpus grows on success and never shrinks silently. Seed it after a green data-check run: node tools/loop/ci-reward.mjs --freeze --log=fetch.log

waiting-list.note: Observed 18/18 months; floor set below observed because the series is rebuilt from 18 separate ~9 MB Incomplete-Provider workbooks and a single transient download timeout would drop one month (still a valid series). 12 = a year of data guaranteed.

rtt-18-week.note: Derived from the same per-provider Incomplete workbooks as waiting-list; same conservative floor.

hmrc-call-wait.note: Observed 23 monthly pts; floor hand-set to 12 (a year). Built by walking ~24 monthly gov.uk editions, so a single transient edition-download drop would lower the count without being a real regression. Floor NOT auto-set to observed for that reason.

cab-foi-intime.note: Observed 12 quarterly pts; floor hand-set to 8 (two years). Walks ~12 quarterly gov.uk editions; conservative floor tolerates a transient edition drop.

desnz-electricity-price.note: Observed 16 annual pts (2010-2025) from a single deterministic QEP ODS (table_224 / sheet 2.2.4). All-or-nothing source, so floor near observed; 14 leaves headroom if a future edition drops a year or two.

defra-sewage-hours.note: Observed 6 annual pts (2020-2025) from the EA EDM zip-of-workbooks. The environment.data.gov.uk host rate-limits/403s intermittently and can serve fewer zips on a bad run, so floor hand-set low to 4 (2020-2023 are the most reliably served). A full host outage makes the whole series SKIP — a known transient, not a code regression.

hmt-food-prices.note: Observed 461 monthly pts (1988-2026) from a deterministic ONS CPI CDID (D7BU). Clean one-liner, very reliable; floor 300 leaves wide headroom while still catching a parser/CDID breakage (which would drop to ~0).

dfe-persistent-absence.note: Observed 6 annual pts (2018/19-2024/25) from the DfE EES absence-by-characteristics data set. Floor hand-set to 4: the EES column names shift by edition and the matched PA% column could move, so leave room while still catching a full break.

mhclg-private-rents.note: Observed 122 monthly pts (2015-2025) from the ONS Price Index of Private Rents historical-series workbook (UK index, Table 1). Floor 60 (5 years) leaves headroom for an edition that republishes a shorter window.

ho-net-migration.note: Observed 55 quarterly rolling-YE pts (2012-2025) from the ONS LTIM provisional publication spreadsheet (Net migration block, col2 total). Floor 24 (6 years): the spreadsheet is republished each release and the metric-block layout can shift, so stay well below observed.

ho-shoplifting.note: Observed 25 annual pts (2002-2025) from ONS Crime in England & Wales appendix Table A5a (police-recorded shoplifting). Floor 12: the big appendix workbook rate-limits (429) intermittently, so leave room for a transient short run.

mhclg-rough-sleeping.note: Observed 16 annual pts (2010-2025) from the MHCLG autumn rough-sleeping snapshot ODS (Table_1_Total). Floor 10 tolerates a future edition trimming early years.

dft-local-roads.note: Observed 18 annual pts (2008-2025) from DfT RDC0121 (% of local 'A' roads needing maintenance), England = mean of the nine English-region rows. Floor 10: the table has no national row so the series depends on the E12 region rows surviving by edition.

dft-rail-fares.note: Observed 449 monthly pts (1989-2026) from the ONS CPI rail-fare CDID (D7EF). Clean deterministic one-liner; floor 300 leaves wide headroom while still catching a CDID/parser break.

mhclg-social-waitlist.note: Observed 39 annual pts (1987-2025) from MHCLG Live Table 600 (households on LA waiting lists), England = sum of the nine English-region rows (no national total row). Floor 20: depends on the E12 region rows surviving by edition.
